institution: World Protocol Academy — Institute for Protocol, Diplomacy, Public Communication & Security Studies

modules.description: Foundational concepts of protocol as a discipline. Covers the definition, historical evolution, and the role of protocol in institutional and diplomatic settings.
modules.symbols_lab_usage: Basic symbol recognition for standard protocol elements (seating, precedence, flag placement).

modules.description: National-level protocol systems, state ceremony, official events, and the hierarchy of state institutions.
primary_sources: Sande Smiljanov — state protocol books and papers
modules.symbols_lab_usage: State institution symbols, hierarchy diagrams, flag precedence charts.

modules.description: Protocol within diplomatic missions and international relations. Covers the Vienna Convention on Diplomatic Relations (1961), precedence among diplomats, and credentials ceremonies.
primary_sources: Selected WPA Working Papers where relevant
modules.symbols_lab_usage: Diplomatic rank symbols, mission type indicators, precedence matrices.

modules.description: Formal dress codes in diplomatic and state settings. Covers morning dress, white tie, black tie, and dress code notation on invitations.
primary_sources: WPA Working Paper No. 001 — Dress Code — In Diplomatic, Business and Military Protocol
Sande Smiljanov — publications on ceremonial dress
modules.symbols_lab_usage: Dress code symbols used on invitations and event programs.

modules.description: Crisis and contingency protocol — managing protocol disruptions, last-minute changes, security incidents, and diplomatic crises.
primary_sources: WPA Working Paper No. 002 — Plan B in Protocol — Contingency Procedures in Diplomatic Practice

modules.description: The complete protocol lifecycle of state visits and official visits. Covers arrival ceremonies, bilateral meetings, state dinners, and departure procedures.
primary_sources: Sande Smiljanov — publications on protocol and diplomacy
WPA WP-007 where relevant to state/official visits to the PRC
modules.symbols_lab_usage: Visit type symbols, ceremony sequence markers, arrival/departure indicators.

modules.description: Protocol as an instrument of soft power. Covers the Protocol Soft Power Index (PSPI), cultural diplomacy through protocol, and national image projection.
primary_sources: WPA WP-008 for qualitative PSPI use and enlargement-protocol case logic
Sande Smiljanov — PSPI-related working papers and doctrine
modules.symbols_lab_usage: PSPI indicator symbols, cultural protocol markers.

modules.title_mk: Самити, мултилатерален протокол и протоколарна геометрија
modules.title_en: Summits, Multilateral Protocol, and Protocol Geometry
modules.description: Protocol for multilateral settings — summits, international conferences, UN assemblies, and the concept of 'protocol geometry' (spatial arrangements reflecting power dynamics).
primary_sources: WPA WP-008 as the main source for 'protocol geometry of enlargement'
WPA WP-007 as a comparative protocol-soft-power case
UN and multilateral protocol sources where approved
modules.symbols_lab_usage: Multilateral seating diagrams, summit symbol set, geometry templates.
